Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows significantly elevated creatinine in both serum and blood, suggesting impaired renal function. Urinalysis reveals marked proteinuria, glucosuria, low specific gravity, and abnormal urine appearance, supporting possible nephropathy. Hemoglobin A1c is low, but urine and blood glucose are normal, making diabetes less likely. Mildly elevated chloride is noted. Recommend urgent nephrology evaluation for possible chronic kidney disease and further assessment of underlying etiology.
